title: artocarpus glaucus var. tephrophyllus
intro: Artocarpus glaucus var. tephrophyllus, also known as the Tephrophyllus jackfruit, is a plant species in the Artocarpus genus, which is native to Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Artocarpus glaucus var. tephrophyllus include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol and blood pressure. Artocarpus glaucus var. tephrophyllus is typically prepared as a tea or decoction, and it can also be used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
